page: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v6 is an x86_64 server instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 virtual CPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations.

This instance is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. While this memory density represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications, the server includes 2835 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. The memory subsystem demonstrates strong performance, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 3685.22 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth at 255.6 GB/sec.

Benchmark data indicates strong computational capabilities. The server achieved a Passmark CPU score of 63378.89 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 20188.0. In specialized workloads, it recorded 67.02 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a static web serving throughput of 81.17 GB/sec. The hardware-accelerated instruction sets contribute to strong image processing and software compilation performance.

Given the low memory-to-CPU ratio combined with substantial local storage, the D48lds_v6 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive RAM footprints. It is suitable for tasks such as web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate-scale LLM inference, where the 2.0 GB per core memory allocation is sufficient.
description: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, representing a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and includes 2835 GB of local storage. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong performance, achieving a Passmark CPU score of 63378.89, a Redis SET throughput of 67.02 million operations per second, and a static web serving throughput of 81.17 GB/sec. This hardware profile is suited for compute-bound workloads, web serving, database operations, and software compilation where high CPU-to-memory density is required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v6 is a compute-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, delivering strong performance in database operations and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48lds_v6 features 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage for compute-heavy and database workloads.
tagline: A compute-focused instance featuring forty-eight Intel Xeon vCPUs, high-speed local storage, and advanced matrix extensions for accelerated workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz.
96.0 GB system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core allocation.
2835 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector and matrix operations.
Strong benchmark results in database operations, Redis throughput, and web serving.
Suited for software compilation, web servers, and compute-bound workloads.
